zh.Prescott, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾普雷斯可特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Parre於1295年出生在英國藍卡爾帕爾時，他的父親Adam D' Parr30歲，母親Margaret Hindley25歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1351年在英國藍卡爾普雷斯可特享年56歲。

zh-hans.Prescott, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾普雷斯可特

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Parre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Perrin
meanings: French and English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Perrin, a pet form of Old French Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perine , Perrine , and Prine .

History: Henri Perrin from Louargat in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Jeanne Merrin in Montreal, QC, in 1661. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variants or altered forms Perrine and Pareyn) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the Huguenot ancestors was Daniel Perrin (1642–1719), nicknamed The Huguenot, who arrived in New York in 1665, aboard the Philip, and settled first in NJ and later on Staten Island. Among his descendants the surname is common in the altered forms Perrine, Perine and Prine. The Staten Island community of Huguenot is named after Daniel Perrin and other Huguenots who settled in the area during the late 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Parre was born in 1295, in Parr, Lancashire, England, his father, Adam D' Parr, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Hindley, was 25. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1351, in Prescot, Lancashire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Parre est né en 1295, à Parr, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Adam D' Parr, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret Hindley, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1351, à Prescot, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Parre 1295 in Parr, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam D' Parr 30 und seine Mutter Margaret Hindley 25. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1351 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Prescot, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Parre è nato il nel 1295 nel Parr, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Adam D' Parr, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Hindley, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1351, nel Prescot, Lancashire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.Prescott, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアプレスコト
ja.Parr Hall, Prescott, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアパール
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Parreが1295年にイギリスランカシュアイアパールで生まれた時，父Adam D' Parrは30歳，母Margaret Hindleyは25歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1351年にイギリスランカシュアイアプレスコトで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Parre(이)가 1295년 영국 랭카실르 파르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam D' Parr(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Hindley(은)는 25세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1351년 영국 랭카실르 프레스컷에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Parre nasceu em 1295, em Parr, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam D' Parr, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Hindley, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1351, em Prescot, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Parre родился в 1295 г. в местности парр, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam D' Parr было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Hindley, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1351 г. в местности прескот, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Parre nació en 1295, en Parr, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam D' Parr, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Margaret Hindley, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1351, en Prescot, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años.
